name: Bouillabaisse
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the fish stock by simmering the heads and bones of white-fleshed fish in 2 to 3 quarts of water for about 2 hours. Strain into a large kettle.
2. Add clam juice, beer, carrots, celery, garlic, leek, and saffron to the strained stock. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer until carrots are crisp-tender.
3. Combine cornstarch with half a cup of white wine in a bowl. Stir into the sauce while it simmers.
4. Simmer for 5 minutes, then season with salt, white pepper, hot pepper sauce, and MSG to taste.
5. Add lobster tails, shrimp (split down the back and sand vein removed), scallops, clams, and halibut fillets to the simmering liquid.
6. Add the remaining wine and bring to a boil.
7. Boil for 8 to 10 minutes or until the lobster is cooked through and clams have opened.
8. Serve in large bowls with generous amounts of liquid. Garlic toast or hot crisp sourdough bread slices can be served alongside if desired.
